title: Some futuristic shit might happen
html:
TL;DR

  • Sep 4 2019, 6:02pm Apple Note titled "Some futuristic shit might happen", body = a com.apple.quicktime-movie attachment reference (the actual video didn't survive export). video-orphan subtype.
  • Title-as-content carries the only signal: 17-year-old Yahir flags a video — almost certainly a clip of something he filmed or screen-recorded — as evidence of "futuristic shit" worth re-watching. Earliest dated future thinking flag in the 2019 corpus.
  • The phrase is sibling-form to later prophetic-mode notes: the Mar 2021 frequency illusion line and the walk with gods eyes / register. 17-yo Yahir is already in I-am-watching-the-future mode.
  • Lossy export pipeline — the .mov is gone, only the metadata pointer remains. Same export-tooling gap as the Nov 21 2021 drawing orphan; pattern reinforces the case for osascript as export tool hardening to capture media-attachment payloads.
  • Graph value: dates Yahir's futurism-flag-as-affect to age 17, adds another row to the media-orphan subtype tally.

Source quote

$CB43E651-FE2C-4786-AD39-830129EC2603com.apple.quicktime-movie

(Body otherwise empty.)

Open questions

  • What was in the video? Recoverable from iCloud / device if the original Apple Note still lives on Yahir's phone.
  • Does the date — Sep 4 2019, 11 days after the first dad premonition — sit in any thematic adjacency? Probably noise; flagged for completeness.
  • Pattern: how many .quicktime-movie orphans across the corpus? Worth a full tally during densify.

Wikilinks
